### Explore the tech stack and tools that power Scribble Network. See what products Scribble Network uses for development, design, marketing, analytics, and more.

Engineering & Development 1

OpenRouter Model Fusion

OpenRouter Model FusionRun many models side by side and fuse the best answer

5.0 (35 reviews)

Unified API

Also considered:

clorocloro

We use both. Cloro runs most of our models (GPT, Gemini, Perplexity, Copilot) and is the more economical option there. We added OpenRouter specifically for Grok, since cloro dropped Grok support, and tracking all five engines reliably is the whole point. Different tool per model.

Finance 1

Stripe

StripeFinancial infrastructure for the internet

4.9 (458 reviews)

Invoicing tools

• [...] 4.9 (458 reviews)

Invoicing tools

•

Payment processors

•

Startup incorporation

Also considered:

PaddlePaddleDodo PaymentsDodo Payments

Stripe just handled the messy parts we didn't want to think about, payments, billing, and multi-currency across the geographies our customers are in. As a small team, that reliability out of the box was worth it. We looked at Dodo payments and Paddle, but Stripe's coverage and developer experience won.

Productivity 1

Figma

FigmaThe collaborative interface design tool

results.title: Scribble Network: The product that makes AI recommend your brand
results.content:
So we built the thing nobody else has: the first AI Search tool married to a creator platform.

We don't just measure your AI visibility, we fix it.

Our product attributes each brand mention back to the exact creator post that earned it.

And the platform now pays creators when they get cited. Creators love this.

This is our edge. We came at this from distribution and not measurement.   
  
We never asked 'how do we track this?', we asked 'how do we actually get brands cited?'  
  
The answer to this already lives in Scribble; try it on your own brand and tell me what you find.

Honest feedback wanted.

My co-founders and I are here all day answering everything.

Mohsin Ali
PicWish

PicWish

@kaavya\_prasad interesting approach with the creator bounties! [...] Hello Fam,

I wanted to give you all context about what we've been building for the last 4 months, and more importantly, show you the data behind it.

We started out as a creator network, building hype and distribution for projects on X. Two years, 50,000+ creators, 100+ campaigns. Then we noticed something: the content our creators made was exactly what AI engines were pulling from when they decided which brands to recommend.

Hey Product Hunt Fam! [...] Amruth Chundi
Scribble Network

Scribble Network

@dipankar\_sarkar Really good point, and no - we don't multi-sample within a single check, it's one call per prompt per day. So you're right that any single day's snapshot has that noise floor baked in.

What we lean on instead is the time dimension: we track each prompt daily, so a single day's citation shift doesn't mean much on its own, but a source holding across multiple consecutive days is a much stronger signal than any one-off result. We treat "cited once" very differently from "cited consistently across a week" — the former could easily be retrieval-layer noise, the latter is much harder to explain away as randomness.
